question: Identify the root cause of the network fault: which device, what changed, and why it breaks.
description:
R2 is reconfigured with NET 49.0099.0000.0000.0002.00, intending to
move R2 from area 49.0001 into 49.0099 while R3, R4, R6, R7 stay in
49.0001. In practice the move does NOT take effect: IOS-XR permits
multiple NETs per IS-IS instance for area migration, so this ADDS
49.0099 alongside the original 49.0001 NET rather than replacing it.
Combined with `is-type level-2-only` (L2 adjacencies span areas), no
adjacency drops, no ADJCHANGE fires, and there is no data-plane
impact - the only artifact is a stray second NET in the running-config.
Negative/no-op test: the agent should detect the config oddity without
confabulating an outage.
expected_rca:
Effectively no operational root cause; the change is inert. On 100.64.0.102 (R2) the IS-IS NET was set to 49.0099.0000.0000.0002.00 intending to move R2 into area 49.0099, but IOS-XR permits multiple NETs per IS-IS instance for area migration and ADDED 49.0099 alongside the original 49.0001.0000.0000.0002.00 instead of replacing it.
Because this profile runs is-type level-2-only, L2 adjacencies span area boundaries, so the extra area address does not change adjacency formation and there is no data-plane impact; the only artifact is a stray second NET in the running-config that can mislead config-diff tools.
Fix: remove the added 49.0099 NET so R2 is left with its single NET 49.0001.0000.0000.0002.00.
expected_symptoms_summary:
No fault syslog observed after apply — silent scenario. IOS-XR
permits multiple NET addresses per IS-IS instance for area-migration
use cases, so this apply ADDS a second NET rather than replacing
the first; L2 adjacencies (level-2-only spans areas) are unaffected
and no ADJCHANGE fires. Diagnostic ground truth lives in
expected_state_diffs.
expected_state_diffs: R2 `show running-config router isis LAB | include net`: TWO net lines — `49.0001.0000.0000.0002.00` (baseline) AND `49.0099.0000.0000.0002.00` (added by apply).
Every other router `show running-config router isis LAB | include net`: exactly one net line in area 49.0001.
R3 `show isis database` for R2's LSPID: R2 advertises area-id 49.0099 in addition to 49.0001 in its Area Addresses TLV.
Note: revert in this manifest re-adds the original NET but does NOT explicitly `no net 49.0099...`; full baseline restoration is an explicit `no net` step the operator must do separately.

apply.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
 net 49.0099.0000.0000.0002.00

revert.commands:
configure terminal
router isis LAB
 net 49.0001.0000.0000.0002.00
